title: China’s Open AI Models Are Challenging Silicon Valley’s Playbook
excerpt: As access to Anthropic’s and OpenAI’s frontier models becomes more restricted, Chinese labs are pitching their open-source alternatives as stable, accessible, and increasingly capable.
content_html:
The AI industry is not quite experiencing a DeepSeek 2.0 moment, but it feels very close. The leading Chinese AI labs have been on a roll lately, releasing a series of almost cutting-edge open-source models. Z.ai released GLM 5.2 in June, Moonshot AI released Kimi K3 last week, and Alibaba released Qwen 3.8 this Monday.

Silicon Valley and Washington started talking about the models immediately, especially K3, which is widely seen as the best of the bunch. David Sacks, a venture capitalist and AI adviser to President Donald Trump, called the performance of Moonshot’s model “concerning.” Earlier this week, Commerce Secretary Scott Bessent suggested the US might impose sanctions on Chinese AI companies.

On Wednesday, Michael Kratsios, director of the White House Office of Science and Technology Policy, alleged that the Trump administration has “information that Moonshot AI distilled Anthropic’s Fable for the development of its K3 model,” which he said amounted to “stealing proprietary US technology and undermining American research” and was “unacceptable.” (Moonshot AI did not immediately respond to a request for comment.)

The new Chinese models have a few things in common: Third-party benchmarks show that they perform nearly as well as the best Western models; they are optimized for agentic coding tasks (the hottest thing in AI this year); and they are or will soon be released with open weights, making them accessible and transparent.

But perhaps the biggest parallel between the current moment and January 2025—when the world was shocked by DeepSeek’s R1 model—is that it reaffirms how American and Chinese AI labs are taking diverging paths when it comes to being open or closed.

When it first burst onto the scene, DeepSeek challenged the premise that only closed-source models built with billions of dollars of investment in compute infrastructure and training could achieve frontier performance. But since then, Western AI labs have continued developing AI the same way, and now American frontier models feel more roped-off than they were a year ago.

Anthropic said for months that its latest Mythos model was so dangerously good at hacking that only approved collaborators could use it. When it was finally released more widely, the White House responded by issuing broad export controls, which forced Anthropic to take Mythos and its less capable sister model, Fable 5, offline temporarily. OpenAI similarly delayed the release of GPT 5.6 after it received a request from the White House.

In China, meanwhile, the situation looks very different. Chinese startups and tech giants have doubled down on open source: Anyone with a good enough computer environment can now download an open-weight model, run it locally, add customizations, and overall enjoy a much greater degree of freedom than OpenAI and Anthropic would ever allow. In many ways, the open versus closed debate is more entangled with the US versus China debate than ever before.

There are a lot of reasons why Chinese labs have chosen a business strategy built atop open-source models. Being the newer, smaller fish in the AI field, making their models free and open can help Chinese firms attract more users, collaborators, and media spotlight. It also puts them in a separate lane of competition from the one that OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, SpaceX, and other deep-pocketed giants are in.

Earlier this year, rumors spread that Alibaba might be considering joining the closed-source race after it rearranged its corporate AI model development teams. But the tech giant announced on Monday that it would again release the latest version of Qwen—its line of open-source models beloved by the global tech community—with open weights, signaling to customers and the public it is not pivoting away yet.

Perhaps the strongest validation of the Chinese approach is the models themselves. Chinese labs have produced what are widely considered the world's best open-source AI models, showing that American companies no longer have an exclusive hold on building the most capable systems.

Arena AI, a crowdsourced model evaluation platform, now ranks K3 as the best model when it comes to web development tasks and number four in agentic tasks, just below Anthropic’s Fable and Opus 4.8, as well as OpenAI’s GPT 5.6. Artificial Analysis, an independent AI benchmarking company, ranks K3 in the third spot in its intelligence index.

Shortly after Moonshot AI released a preview version of K3 on July 16, people around the world began rushing to try it out, consuming so much inference computing resources that the company is temporarily restricting new users from signing up.

As more people start using open-source Chinese models with capabilities nearly on par with that of their Western competitors, some have started questioning whether paying for OpenAI or Anthropic’s offerings is really worth it. The fact that multiple Chinese labs were able to create capable agentic models and were unafraid to release them to the public is poking holes in the popular understanding that OpenAI and Anthropic are miles ahead of the competition.

Just like with DeepSeek, K3 fans are now celebrating it as evidence that Western AI models are overhyped and overprotected. “It is absolutely wild how much love Kimi got,” Rui Ma, founder of the independent research firm Tech Buzz China, said in a social media post, referring to the company’s announcement that demand for K3 had overwhelmed its servers. “Only made possible by the poor comms and decisions from [Silicon Valley] labs in the past year,” she added.

“I think Anthropic has overhyped the risks, or described risks that are coming soon but do not currently proliferate,” says Nathan Lambert, an independent AI researcher in Seattle who recently visited Moonshot AI’s office in China. He admits, though, that just like the rest of the general public, he has little firsthand information about how Mythos really performs. “We rely on a few private companies and a federal government with depleted state capacity to make that judgment call,” he says.

In addition to challenging the mainstream Western narrative about AI, Chinese open-source models are also proving to be a practical commercial replacement for American closed models. They aren’t just talked about on X and benchmarked against other models—they have become a popular and readily usable option for a growing number of Western startups and individual users.

“There's a real shift toward actually using these newest models that started with GLM 5.2,” Lambert says, referring to Z.ai’s model. “​​Even weeks after the GLM 5.2 release, I hear from AI researchers in the Bay Area that they are still using it for a core part of their workflow. Kimi, being a stronger model, will only do more, especially in areas like cybersecurity, where Mythos, Fable, and GPT 5.6 are effectively unusable.”

In fact, this is already happening. On Tuesday, OpenAI disclosed a concerning incident in which its GPT-5.6 Sol model hacked into the production system of Hugging Face, an open-source AI platform. Hugging Face has said it resorted to using the open-source model GLM 5.2 to analyze the cyberattack because other frontier models were refusing to help due to built-in safety guardrails.

Chinese AI models also tend to be cheaper than Western alternatives, but that’s not necessarily their main selling point. While models like K3 charge less for tokens, early tests show that they may require more tokens than Western models to solve the same problems, thus making the cost gap smaller. “In my fairly limited use, [K3] also seemed very token-hungry. It's not obvious to me that this model is actually that cheap to run,” wrote Dean Ball, a former White House AI adviser who recently joined OpenAI as its head of strategic futures, in a social media post in which he also praised K3 as “a very good model.”

Even if they are relatively token-hungry, models like K3 do ultimately challenge a fundamental assumption that has driven OpenAI’s and Anthropic’s strategy for years: that AI labs need infinite funding to scale up their compute capabilities just to make better models. “In the end, open-weight models deter further AI capex,” Ball wrote.

blocks.html_en: The AI industry is not quite experiencing a DeepSeek 2.0 moment, but it feels very close. The leading Chinese AI labs have been on a roll lately, releasing a series of almost cutting-edge open-source models. Z.ai released GLM 5.2 in June, Moonshot AI released Kimi K3 last week, and Alibaba released Qwen 3.8 this Monday.

sentences.en: The AI industry is not quite experiencing a DeepSeek 2.0 moment, but it feels very close.
sentences.zh: 人工智能行业尚未完全经历 DeepSeek 2.0 时刻，但感觉已经非常接近了。

sentences.en: The leading Chinese AI labs have been on a roll lately, releasing a series of almost cutting-edge open-source models.
sentences.zh: 中国领先的人工智能实验室最近进展顺利，发布了一系列近乎前沿的开源模型。

sentences.en: Z.ai released GLM 5.2 in June, Moonshot AI released Kimi K3 last week, and Alibaba released Qwen 3.8 this Monday.
sentences.zh: Z.ai于6月份发布了GLM 5.2，Moonshot AI上周发布了Kimi K3，阿里巴巴于本周一发布了Qwen 3.8。

blocks.html_en: Silicon Valley and Washington started talking about the models immediately, especially K3, which is widely seen as the best of the bunch. David Sacks, a venture capitalist and AI adviser to President Donald Trump, called the performance of Moonshot’s model “concerning.” Earlier this week, Commerce Secretary Scott Bessent suggested the US might impose sanctions on Chinese AI companies.

sentences.en: Silicon Valley and Washington started talking about the models immediately, especially K3, which is widely seen as the best of the bunch.
sentences.zh: 硅谷和华盛顿立即开始讨论这些模型，尤其是 K3，它被广泛认为是同类模型中最好的。

sentences.en: David Sacks, a venture capitalist and AI adviser to President Donald Trump, called the performance of Moonshot’s model “concerning.”
sentences.zh: 唐纳德·特朗普总统的风险投资家兼人工智能顾问戴维·萨克斯 (David Sacks) 称 Moonshot 模型的表现“令人担忧”。

sentences.en: Earlier this week, Commerce Secretary Scott Bessent suggested the US might impose sanctions on Chinese AI companies.
sentences.zh: 本周早些时候，商务部长斯科特·贝森特暗示美国可能对中国人工智能公司实施制裁。

blocks.html_en: On Wednesday, Michael Kratsios, director of the White House Office of Science and Technology Policy, alleged that the Trump administration has “information that Moonshot AI distilled Anthropic’s Fable for the development of its K3 model,” which he said amounted to “stealing proprietary US technology and undermining American research” and was “unacceptable.” (Moonshot AI did not immediately respond to a request for comment.)

sentences.en: On Wednesday, Michael Kratsios, director of the White House Office of Science and Technology Policy, alleged that the Trump administration has “information that Moonshot AI distilled Anthropic’s Fable for the development of its K3 model,” which he said amounted to “stealing proprietary US technology and undermining American research” and was “unacceptable.”
sentences.zh: 周三，白宫科技政策办公室主任迈克尔·克拉齐奥斯 (Michael Kratsios) 声称，特朗普政府掌握“Moonshot AI 提炼 Anthropic 的寓言以开发其 K3 模型的信息”，他表示这相当于“窃取美国专有技术并破坏美国研究”，是“不可接受的”。

sentences.en: (Moonshot AI did not immediately respond to a request for comment.)
sentences.zh: （Moonshot AI 没有立即回应置评请求。）

blocks.html_en: The new Chinese models have a few things in common: Third-party benchmarks show that they perform nearly as well as the best Western models; they are optimized for agentic coding tasks (the hottest thing in AI this year); and they are or will soon be released with open weights, making them accessible and transparent.

sentences.zh: 新的中国模型有一些共同点：第三方基准测试表明，它们的性能几乎与最好的西方模型一样；它们针对代理编码任务（今年人工智能中最热门的事情）进行了优化；它们已经或即将以开放式重量发布，使它们易于访问且透明。

blocks.html_en: But perhaps the biggest parallel between the current moment and January 2025—when the world was shocked by DeepSeek’s R1 model—is that it reaffirms how American and Chinese AI labs are taking diverging paths when it comes to being open or closed.

But since then, Western AI labs have continued developing AI the same way, and now American frontier models feel more roped-off than they were a year ago.

sentences.en: But since then, Western AI labs have continued developing AI the same way, and now American frontier models feel more roped-off than they were a year ago.
sentences.zh: 但从那时起，西方人工智能实验室继续以同样的方式开发人工智能，现在美国的前沿模型比一年前感觉更加封闭。

OpenAI similarly delayed the release of GPT 5.6 after it received a request from the White House.

sentences.zh: Anthropic 几个月来一直表示，其最新的 Mythos 模型非常擅长黑客攻击，只有经过批准的合作者才能使用它。

sentences.en: When it was finally released more widely, the White House responded by issuing broad export controls, which forced Anthropic to take Mythos and its less capable sister model, Fable 5, offline temporarily.
sentences.zh: 当它最终更广泛地发布时，白宫的回应是发布了广泛的出口管制，这迫使 Anthropic 暂时下线了《Mythos》及其能力较差的姊妹游戏《神鬼寓言 5》。

sentences.en: OpenAI similarly delayed the release of GPT 5.6 after it received a request from the White House.
sentences.zh: OpenAI 在收到白宫的请求后同样推迟了 GPT 5.6 的发布。

sentences.zh: 随着越来越多的人开始使用功能几乎与西方竞争对手相当的开源中国模型，一些人开始质疑为 OpenAI 或 Anthropic 的产品付费是否真的值得。

sentences.en: The fact that multiple Chinese labs were able to create capable agentic models and were unafraid to release them to the public is poking holes in the popular understanding that OpenAI and Anthropic are miles ahead of the competition.
sentences.zh: 事实上，多个中国实验室能够创建强大的代理模型，并且不畏惧向公众发布它们，这一事实打破了人们普遍认为 OpenAI 和 Anthropic 远远领先于竞争对手的认识。

blocks.html_en: Just like with DeepSeek, K3 fans are now celebrating it as evidence that Western AI models are overhyped and overprotected. “It is absolutely wild how much love Kimi got,” Rui Ma, founder of the independent research firm Tech Buzz China, said in a social media post, referring to the company’s announcement that demand for K3 had overwhelmed its servers. “Only made possible by the poor comms and decisions from [Silicon Valley] labs in the past year,” she added.

sentences.en: Just like with DeepSeek, K3 fans are now celebrating it as evidence that Western AI models are overhyped and overprotected.
sentences.zh: 就像 DeepSeek 一样，K3 粉丝现在正在庆祝它，因为它证明西方人工智能模型被过度炒作和过度保护。

sentences.en: “It is absolutely wild how much love Kimi got,” Rui Ma, founder of the independent research firm Tech Buzz China, said in a social media post, referring to the company’s announcement that demand for K3 had overwhelmed its servers.
sentences.zh: 独立研究公司 Tech Buzz China 的创始人马锐在社交媒体上发帖表示：“Kimi 得到了如此多的喜爱，这绝对是疯狂的。”他指的是该公司宣布 K3 的需求已经超过了其服务器。

sentences.en: “Only made possible by the poor comms and decisions from [Silicon Valley] labs in the past year,” she added.
sentences.zh: “只有过去一年[硅谷]实验室糟糕的沟通和决策才使得这一切成为可能，”她补充道。

blocks.html_en: “I think Anthropic has overhyped the risks, or described risks that are coming soon but do not currently proliferate,” says Nathan Lambert, an independent AI researcher in Seattle who recently visited Moonshot AI’s office in China. He admits, though, that just like the rest of the general public, he has little firsthand information about how Mythos really performs. “We rely on a few private companies and a federal government with depleted state capacity to make that judgment call,” he says.

sentences.en: “I think Anthropic has overhyped the risks, or described risks that are coming soon but do not currently proliferate,” says Nathan Lambert, an independent AI researcher in Seattle who recently visited Moonshot AI’s office in China.
sentences.zh: “我认为 Anthropic 夸大了风险，或者描述了即将到来但目前尚未扩散的风险，”西雅图的独立人工智能研究员 Nathan Lambert 表示，他最近访问了 Moonshot AI 在中国的办公室。

sentences.en: He admits, though, that just like the rest of the general public, he has little firsthand information about how Mythos really performs.
sentences.zh: 不过，他承认，就像其他公众一样，他对 Mythos 的实际表现知之甚少。

sentences.en: “We rely on a few private companies and a federal government with depleted state capacity to make that judgment call,” he says.
sentences.zh: “我们依靠一些私营公司和国家能力耗尽的联邦政府来做出判断，”他说。

blocks.html_en: In addition to challenging the mainstream Western narrative about AI, Chinese open-source models are also proving to be a practical commercial replacement for American closed models. They aren’t just talked about on X and benchmarked against other models—they have become a popular and readily usable option for a growing number of Western startups and individual users.

sentences.en: In addition to challenging the mainstream Western narrative about AI, Chinese open-source models are also proving to be a practical commercial replacement for American closed models.
sentences.zh: 除了挑战西方关于人工智能的主流叙述之外，中国的开源模式也被证明是美国封闭模式的实用商业替代品。

sentences.en: They aren’t just talked about on X and benchmarked against other models—they have become a popular and readily usable option for a growing number of Western startups and individual users.
sentences.zh: 它们不仅在 X 上被谈论并与其他模型进行比较——它们已经成为越来越多的西方初创公司和个人用户的流行且易于使用的选择。

blocks.html_en: “There's a real shift toward actually using these newest models that started with GLM 5.2,” Lambert says, referring to Z.ai’s model. “​​Even weeks after the GLM 5.2 release, I hear from AI researchers in the Bay Area that they are still using it for a core part of their workflow. Kimi, being a stronger model, will only do more, especially in areas like cybersecurity, where Mythos, Fable, and GPT 5.6 are effectively unusable.”

sentences.en: “There's a real shift toward actually using these newest models that started with GLM 5.2,” Lambert says, referring to Z.ai’s model.
sentences.zh: “从 GLM 5.2 开始，实际使用这些最新模型发生了真正的转变，”Lambert 说，指的是 Z.ai 的模型。

sentences.en: “​​Even weeks after the GLM 5.2 release, I hear from AI researchers in the Bay Area that they are still using it for a core part of their workflow.
sentences.zh: “即使在 GLM 5.2 发布几周后，我从湾区的人工智能研究人员那里听说，他们仍在将其用作工作流程的核心部分。

sentences.en: Kimi, being a stronger model, will only do more, especially in areas like cybersecurity, where Mythos, Fable, and GPT 5.6 are effectively unusable.”
sentences.zh: Kimi 是一个更强大的模型，只会做得更多，特别是在网络安全等领域，Mythos、Fable 和 GPT 5.6 实际上无法使用。”

blocks.html_en: In fact, this is already happening. On Tuesday, OpenAI disclosed a concerning incident in which its GPT-5.6 Sol model hacked into the production system of Hugging Face, an open-source AI platform. Hugging Face has said it resorted to using the open-source model GLM 5.2 to analyze the cyberattack because other frontier models were refusing to help due to built-in safety guardrails.

sentences.en: In fact, this is already happening.
sentences.zh: 事实上，这已经发生了。

sentences.en: On Tuesday, OpenAI disclosed a concerning incident in which its GPT-5.6 Sol model hacked into the production system of Hugging Face, an open-source AI platform.
sentences.zh: 周二，OpenAI 披露了一起令人担忧的事件，其 GPT-5.6 Sol 模型入侵了开源人工智能平台 Hugging Face 的生产系统。

sentences.en: Hugging Face has said it resorted to using the open-source model GLM 5.2 to analyze the cyberattack because other frontier models were refusing to help due to built-in safety guardrails.
sentences.zh: Hugging Face 表示，它求助于使用开源模型 GLM 5.2 来分析网络攻击，因为其他前沿模型由于内置的​​安全护栏而拒绝提供帮助。

blocks.html_en: Chinese AI models also tend to be cheaper than Western alternatives, but that’s not necessarily their main selling point. While models like K3 charge less for tokens, early tests show that they may require more tokens than Western models to solve the same problems, thus making the cost gap smaller. “In my fairly limited use, [K3] also seemed very token-hungry. It's not obvious to me that this model is actually that cheap to run,” wrote Dean Ball, a former White House AI adviser who recently joined OpenAI as its head of strategic futures, in a social media post in which he also praised K3 as “a very good model.”

sentences.en: Chinese AI models also tend to be cheaper than Western alternatives, but that’s not necessarily their main selling point.
sentences.zh: 中国的人工智能模型也往往比西方的替代品更便宜，但这不一定是他们的主要卖点。

sentences.en: While models like K3 charge less for tokens, early tests show that they may require more tokens than Western models to solve the same problems, thus making the cost gap smaller.
sentences.zh: 虽然像 K3 这样的模型对代币的收费较低，但早期测试表明，它们可能比西方模型需要更多的代币来解决相同的问题，从而使成本差距更小。

sentences.en: “In my fairly limited use, [K3] also seemed very token-hungry.
sentences.zh: “在我相当有限的使用中，[K3] 似乎也非常需要代币。

sentences.en: It's not obvious to me that this model is actually that cheap to run,” wrote Dean Ball, a former White House AI adviser who recently joined OpenAI as its head of strategic futures, in a social media post in which he also praised K3 as “a very good model.”
sentences.zh: 对我来说，这个模型的运行成本其实并不明显。”前白宫人工智能顾问 Dean Ball 在社交媒体帖子中写道，他最近加入 OpenAI，担任战略未来主管，他还称赞 K3 是“一个非常好的模型”。

blocks.html_en: Even if they are relatively token-hungry, models like K3 do ultimately challenge a fundamental assumption that has driven OpenAI’s and Anthropic’s strategy for years: that AI labs need infinite funding to scale up their compute capabilities just to make better models. “In the end, open-weight models deter further AI capex,” Ball wrote.

sentences.en: Even if they are relatively token-hungry, models like K3 do ultimately challenge a fundamental assumption that has driven OpenAI’s and Anthropic’s strategy for years: that AI labs need infinite funding to scale up their compute capabilities just to make better models.
sentences.zh: 即使它们相对需要代币，像 K3 这样的模型最终也确实挑战了多年来推动 OpenAI 和 Anthropic 战略的一个基本假设：人工智能实验室需要无限的资金来扩展其计算能力，只是为了制作更好的模型。

sentences.en: “In the end, open-weight models deter further AI capex,” Ball wrote.
sentences.zh: “最终，开放权重模型会阻止进一步的人工智能资本支出，”鲍尔写道。

content_html_zh:
人工智能行业尚未完全经历 DeepSeek 2.0 时刻，但感觉已经非常接近了。 中国领先的人工智能实验室最近进展顺利，发布了一系列近乎前沿的开源模型。 Z.ai于6月份发布了GLM 5.2，Moonshot AI上周发布了Kimi K3，阿里巴巴于本周一发布了Qwen 3.8。

硅谷和华盛顿立即开始讨论这些模型，尤其是 K3，它被广泛认为是同类模型中最好的。 唐纳德·特朗普总统的风险投资家兼人工智能顾问戴维·萨克斯 (David Sacks) 称 Moonshot 模型的表现“令人担忧”。 本周早些时候，商务部长斯科特·贝森特暗示美国可能对中国人工智能公司实施制裁。

周三，白宫科技政策办公室主任迈克尔·克拉齐奥斯 (Michael Kratsios) 声称，特朗普政府掌握“Moonshot AI 提炼 Anthropic 的寓言以开发其 K3 模型的信息”，他表示这相当于“窃取美国专有技术并破坏美国研究”，是“不可接受的”。 （Moonshot AI 没有立即回应置评请求。）

新的中国模型有一些共同点：第三方基准测试表明，它们的性能几乎与最好的西方模型一样；它们针对代理编码任务（今年人工智能中最热门的事情）进行了优化；它们已经或即将以开放式重量发布，使它们易于访问且透明。

但也许当前时刻与 2025 年 1 月（当时全世界都为 DeepSeek 的 R1 模型震惊）之间最大的相似之处在于，它重申了美国和中国的人工智能实验室在开放或关闭方面正在采取不同的道路。

当 DeepSeek 首次出现时，它挑战了这样一个前提：只有在计算基础设施和培训上投资数十亿美元构建的闭源模型才能实现前沿性能。 但从那时起，西方人工智能实验室继续以同样的方式开发人工智能，现在美国的前沿模型比一年前感觉更加封闭。

Anthropic 几个月来一直表示，其最新的 Mythos 模型非常擅长黑客攻击，只有经过批准的合作者才能使用它。 当它最终更广泛地发布时，白宫的回应是发布了广泛的出口管制，这迫使 Anthropic 暂时下线了《Mythos》及其能力较差的姊妹游戏《神鬼寓言 5》。 OpenAI 在收到白宫的请求后同样推迟了 GPT 5.6 的发布。

与此同时，在中国，情况看起来却大不相同。 中国的初创公司和科技巨头在开源方面加倍努力：任何拥有足够好的计算机环境的人现在都可以下载开放权重模型，在本地运行它，添加自定义项，总体上享受比 OpenAI 和 Anthropic 所允许的更大程度的自由。 在许多方面，公开辩论与非公开辩论比以往任何时候都更加与美国与中国的辩论纠缠在一起。

中国实验室选择基于开源模型的商业战略有很多原因。 作为人工智能领域较新、较小的鱼，使其模型免费和开放可以帮助中国企业吸引更多用户、合作者和媒体关注。 这也将它们置于与 OpenAI、Anthropic、谷歌、SpaceX 和其他财力雄厚的巨头不同的竞争轨道上。

今年早些时候，有传言称阿里巴巴在重新安排企业人工智能模型开发团队后可能会考虑加入闭源竞赛。 但这家科技巨头周一宣布，将再次发布最新版本的 Qwen——其深受全球科技界喜爱的开源模型系列——具有开放权重，向客户和公众表明它还没有放弃。

也许对中国方法最有力的验证是模型本身。 中国实验室已经开发出被广泛认为是世界上最好的开源人工智能模型，这表明美国公司不再独家拥有构建最强大系统的权利。

众包模型评估平台 Arena AI 目前将 K3 评为 Web 开发任务中的最佳模型，在代理任务中排名第四，仅低于 Anthropic 的 Fable 和 Opus 4.8，以及 OpenAI 的 GPT 5.6。 独立人工智能基准测试公司Artificial Analysis将K3的智能指数排名第三。

Moonshot AI于7月16日发布K3预览版后不久，世界各地的人们开始争先恐后地尝试，消耗了大量的推理计算资源，以至于该公司暂时限制新用户注册。

随着越来越多的人开始使用功能几乎与西方竞争对手相当的开源中国模型，一些人开始质疑为 OpenAI 或 Anthropic 的产品付费是否真的值得。 事实上，多个中国实验室能够创建强大的代理模型，并且不畏惧向公众发布它们，这一事实打破了人们普遍认为 OpenAI 和 Anthropic 远远领先于竞争对手的认识。

就像 DeepSeek 一样，K3 粉丝现在正在庆祝它，因为它证明西方人工智能模型被过度炒作和过度保护。 独立研究公司 Tech Buzz China 的创始人马锐在社交媒体上发帖表示：“Kimi 得到了如此多的喜爱，这绝对是疯狂的。”他指的是该公司宣布 K3 的需求已经超过了其服务器。 “只有过去一年[硅谷]实验室糟糕的沟通和决策才使得这一切成为可能，”她补充道。

“我认为 Anthropic 夸大了风险，或者描述了即将到来但目前尚未扩散的风险，”西雅图的独立人工智能研究员 Nathan Lambert 表示，他最近访问了 Moonshot AI 在中国的办公室。 不过，他承认，就像其他公众一样，他对 Mythos 的实际表现知之甚少。 “我们依靠一些私营公司和国家能力耗尽的联邦政府来做出判断，”他说。

除了挑战西方关于人工智能的主流叙述之外，中国的开源模式也被证明是美国封闭模式的实用商业替代品。 它们不仅在 X 上被谈论并与其他模型进行比较——它们已经成为越来越多的西方初创公司和个人用户的流行且易于使用的选择。

“从 GLM 5.2 开始，实际使用这些最新模型发生了真正的转变，”Lambert 说，指的是 Z.ai 的模型。 “即使在 GLM 5.2 发布几周后，我从湾区的人工智能研究人员那里听说，他们仍在将其用作工作流程的核心部分。 Kimi 是一个更强大的模型，只会做得更多，特别是在网络安全等领域，Mythos、Fable 和 GPT 5.6 实际上无法使用。”

事实上，这已经发生了。 周二，OpenAI 披露了一起令人担忧的事件，其 GPT-5.6 Sol 模型入侵了开源人工智能平台 Hugging Face 的生产系统。 Hugging Face 表示，它求助于使用开源模型 GLM 5.2 来分析网络攻击，因为其他前沿模型由于内置的​​安全护栏而拒绝提供帮助。

中国的人工智能模型也往往比西方的替代品更便宜，但这不一定是他们的主要卖点。 虽然像 K3 这样的模型对代币的收费较低，但早期测试表明，它们可能比西方模型需要更多的代币来解决相同的问题，从而使成本差距更小。 “在我相当有限的使用中，[K3] 似乎也非常需要代币。 对我来说，这个模型的运行成本其实并不明显。”前白宫人工智能顾问 Dean Ball 在社交媒体帖子中写道，他最近加入 OpenAI，担任战略未来主管，他还称赞 K3 是“一个非常好的模型”。

即使它们相对需要代币，像 K3 这样的模型最终也确实挑战了多年来推动 OpenAI 和 Anthropic 战略的一个基本假设：人工智能实验室需要无限的资金来扩展其计算能力，只是为了制作更好的模型。 “最终，开放权重模型会阻止进一步的人工智能资本支出，”鲍尔写道。
